title: Unvalidated Open Redirect in Login Redirection Parameter

steps.message: Block 'http://attacker.com' at the WAF or firewall to prevent further malicious redirects, as per general incident containment principles for identified malicious targets.

Query SIEM logs for instances of '/login?redirect=http://attacker.com' or similar unvalidated redirects to assess the scope and impact of the attack, reviewing how user-controllable data is incorporated into the URL to find any bypasses to validation, as outlined in the KB mitigation.

Temporarily disable or sanitize the 'redirect' parameter on the '/login' endpoint to prevent further exploitation of the open redirect vulnerability, aligning with the KB's guidance to review how user-controllable data is incorporated and ensure validation exists.

Approve the deployment of a permanent code fix to implement stringent URL validation and whitelisting for all redirect parameters, as described in the KB mitigation to determine how user-controllable data is incorporated and ensure validation.

params.fix_details: Implement URL validation/whitelisting for redirect parameter.

steps.message: Create a high-priority ticket in Jira for the development team to implement robust server-side validation and whitelisting for all redirect parameters, specifically addressing the '/login' endpoint's 'redirect' parameter, to prevent recurrence of unvalidated redirects, as the KB stresses the importance of validation.

params.description: Implement URL validation and whitelisting for redirect parameters.

steps.message: Send a notification to the SOC communications channel summarizing the open redirect incident, immediate containment actions, and status of the permanent fix implementation.

params.message: Open Redirect incident on /login endpoint contained. Investigation ongoing, permanent fix initiated.
